title: Print subarray with maximum subarray sum

content.problemStatement:
Given an integer array numsnumsnums, find the longest contiguous subarray (containing at least one element) with the largest sum and **print the elements of that subarray**.

Note:If two or more sub arrays have the same length and same max sum then you should return the leftmost array.

**Function Declaration****Function Name**maxSubArraymaxSubArraymaxSubArray – This function finds the **longest contiguous subarray** (with at least one element) that has the **maximum possible sum**, and returns the elements of that subarray.
If multiple subarrays have the **same maximum sum and same length**, the function returns the **leftmost** such subarray.

**Parameters**

numsnumsnums : A reference to a vector of integers representing the array.



Each element can be negative, zero, or positive.


**Return Value**

Returns an array containing the elements of the contiguous subarray that:



Has the **maximum subarray sum**.
If multiple subarrays achieve this sum, the function returns the **longest** one.
If still tied, returns the **leftmost** such subarray.


**Constraints**
1≤nums.length≤1051 \leq nums.length \leq 10^51≤nums.length≤105
−104≤nums[i]≤104-10^4 \leq nums[i] \leq 10^4−104≤nums[i]≤104
The solution must run in **O(N)** time due to input size.

Input Format


The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.




For each test case:



The first line contains an integer NNN — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the array elements.




Output Format

For each test case, print the elements of the **longest maximum-sum subarray** on a new line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the elements of the longest contiguous subarray having the maximum possible sum. Elements are printed in their original order separated by a single space. Print a newline after each test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the subarray from index 0 to 3 has the largest sum = 6. For the second test case, the subarray from index 0 to 4 has the largest sum = 5.
The subarray with the largest sum is [4].
The subarray with the largest sum is the entire array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].

solution.approach:
We use a modified Kadane’s algorithm. While scanning the array we keep:
- maxEndingHere: maximum sum of a subarray that ends at the current index.
- maxSoFar: best sum seen so far.
- start, end: indices of the best subarray.
- tempStart: potential start index for the current maxEndingHere.
When extending the current subarray gives a sum not larger than starting a new subarray at the current element, we start a new subarray (tempStart = i). After updating maxEndingHere we compare it with maxSoFar. If it is larger, we record the new best interval. If it is equal, we prefer the longer interval (i - tempStart > end - start). If lengths are also equal, the earlier (leftmost) interval is already stored, so we keep it. The algorithm runs in O(N) time and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array.
